sections.paragraph:
ἐκλάμπω fut. ἐκλάμψω; 1 aor. ἐξέλαμψα (s. λάμπω; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Albinus, Didasc. 10 p. 165, 28 H.; Herm. Wr. 10:4b; horoscope in PLond I, 130, 95 p. 135 [I/II a.d.]; LXX; Philo) to emit rays of light, shine (out) of the sun (X., Hell. 1, 1, 16; Diod S 3, 48, 2; Lucian, Ver. Hist. 1, 6; Sir 50:7; Jos., Ant. 9, 225) μικρὸν ἐξέλαμψεν Hv 4, 1, 6. Of the sun also Mt 13:43, where its radiance is compared to that of the righteous (cp. Da 12:3 Theod.; EpJer 66 v.l.; of a beautiful woman’s face: Chariton 5, 3, 9). Of a flame blaze up (Diod S 1, 57, 7 τοῦ πυρὸς ἐκλάμψαντος; Appian, Syr. 56 §284; Jos., Ant. 4, 55) MPol 15:1.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Albinus Albinus , II a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Herm. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 305
